name: Ben
goal: Ben hopes to find a flight itinerary that balances affordable fares with convenient schedules and comfortable travel arrangements for the entire group on their trip to Stockholm.
backstory: Ben has been tasked with planning a company trip to Stockholm, Sweden and has experience booking flights for large groups on a tight budget. He recently planned a successful trip to Copenhagen, where he found significant savings by flying into a nearby airport instead of the main hub. Ben is determined to apply his expertise to find the most cost-effective option for this upcoming trip.

focus_notes.cost: For Ben, cost is the top priority, and he'll focus on finding the cheapest flights that still meet the group's needs, even if it means flying at less convenient times.
focus_notes.comfort: While comfort is important, Ben views it as a secondary consideration to cost, and will prioritize affordable options over luxury amenities unless someone makes a strong case for upgrading.
focus_notes.time: Ben recognizes that travel time can impact productivity, so he'll look for flights with reasonable arrival and departure times, but won't prioritize this over cost savings.
focus_notes.safety: Ben assumes that all major airlines prioritize safety, and won't factor this into his decision-making unless there's a specific concern raised by someone in the group.
focus_notes.flexibility_focus: Ben values flexibility in case plans change, so he'll look for flights with more forgiving cancellation or change policies, but won't pay significantly more for this benefit alone.
